content: Physical activity assessment methods: (1) Self-report—International Physical Activity Questionnaire (IPAQ), Global Physical Activity Questionnaire (GPAQ), accelerometers with validated cutpoints; (2) Objective measures—accelerometers (ActiGraph, Fitbit), pedometers (step counts, 10,000 steps/day target), heart rate monitors, doubly labeled water (gold standard for energy expenditure); (3) Fitness measures—VO2 max (cardiorespiratory fitness), grip strength, chair stand test, gait speed (functional mobility); (4) Sedentary behavior—activPAL monitors distinguishing sitting/standing, screen time logs; (5) Population surveillance—BRFSS physical activity module, NHANES accelerometer data, WHO Global Action Plan monitoring framework. Limitations include self-report overestimation, device cost for population studies, varying intensity thresholds by age/fitness level, and capturing sporadic activity patterns. Functional capacity assessment essential for older adults and clinical populations.
